settings.title: Tag Group Manager settings
settings.selectAtLeastOneTag: Please select at least one tag
settings.deleteGroupConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this group?
settings.tip1: Find the star icon in the left sidebar and click it to open the sort/insert quick page in the right sidebar area.
settings.tip2: Switch between sort/insert modes by clicking on tag group names. Drag handles appear above tag group names only in sort mode for reordering.
settings.tip3: After adding new tags to a tag group in settings, you need to click the tag group name to refresh and display them.
settings.tip4: You must be in an editable view to invoke individual tag group commands from the command palette.
settings.tip5: After adding new tags to a tag group in settings, shift+click the cycle icon in the floating tag selector to refresh.
settings.tip6: In non-cycle mode, tags become grayed out after being inserted once, making it ideal for selective insertion scenarios with a defined tag scope.
settings.languageAutoDetected: Language automatically detected based on Obsidian's language settings
settings.enableCustomColorsDesc: Adds soft gradient backgrounds to all tags when enabled; colors are customizable. Restores default style when disabled. Toggling does not affect color data
settings.colorMappingDesc: Set custom colors for tags. Supports exact matching and regex matching.
settings.addColorMappingDesc: Add color mapping for tag names or regex patterns
settings.useRegexDesc: Enable to treat pattern as regex
settings.singleTagColorSettingDesc: Click tags in tag groups below to set colors
settings.batchColorOperationDesc: Supports plain text and regex matching
settings.setGroupColorDesc: Apply the same color to all tags in this group
settings.groupColorApplied: Color applied to {count} tags in {groupName} group

overview.title: Tag overview
overview.insertModeTitle: Insert mode - click tags to insert
overview.doubleClickToSwitch: Double-click to switch between insert and sort modes

commands.insertHere: Please click on the tag to insert

messages.duplicateTag: Tag already exists in this group
messages.noEditorFound: No editor found, please open a document first
messages.openMarkdownFirst: Please open a Markdown document and place the cursor at the insertion point
messages.tagGroupUpdated: Tag group updated, added {count} new tags
messages.tagGroupUpToDate: Tag group is up to date
messages.cycleButtonTooltip: Cyclic mode: off — click to turn on cyclic mode to restore all tags and keep the number the same. Shift+click to update the tag list from the tag group.
messages.cycleButtonTooltip1: Cyclic mode: enabled — click to turn off cyclic mode. Shift+click to update the tag list from the tag group.
messages.cycleButtonTooltip2: Cyclic mode: off — click to turn on cyclic mode to restore all tags. Shift+click to update the tag list from the tag group.
messages.cannotInsertInSortMode: Tag insertion is disabled in Sort Mode. Please disable sorting to switch back to Insert Mode.

rename.title: Rename Tag
rename.oldTagNameDesc: Enter the tag name to replace (without #)
rename.includeCanvasDesc: Also replace tags in Canvas text cards and groups (Experimental)
rename.warning: This will modify files across your vault. Please ensure you have a backup!

tagGroupSets.title: Tag group sets
tagGroupSets.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this group set?

multiLevelAdaptation.title: Multi-level Tag Adaptation
multiLevelAdaptation.autoExpandDesc: Automatically convert multi-level tags into Sets (Level 1) and Groups (Level 2).
multiLevelAdaptation.insertionModeDesc: Choose how multi-level tags are inserted.
multiLevelAdaptation.enableStylingDesc: Apply configured colors to tags in Editor and Preview modes.

smartMatch.title: Smart Multi-level Tag Matching
smartMatch.desc: Works with auto-expand feature to automatically add new multi-level tags to corresponding tag groups (only processes unassigned tags)
smartMatch.enabledDesc: When enabled, new multi-level tags will be automatically matched to tag groups by hierarchy
smartMatch.modeNote: Match mode is automatically set based on expand depth. Tags won't be added if no matching group is found
smartMatch.createIfNotExistsDesc: Whether to automatically create tag groups if they don't exist (not yet implemented)
